node.title: Just sharing my journey, please read only if you have patience

article.title: Just sharing my journey, please read only if you have patience
article.content:
# Feeling completely stuck… need guidance from seniors

Hi bhaiya / didi,

I don’t usually write posts but today I really felt I should.

I’m a 2024 pass out. Honestly my college placements were almost dead. Hardly any companies came and I passed out completely jobless. Watching everyone on LinkedIn post joining updates while I was sitting at home was already painful.

After some time I somehow got an opening at a very very small startup. Team of just 4 people. The CTO was 21 years old, extremely arrogant, toxic culture, shouting for small things, chain smoking whole day. Late nights were kind of compulsory there.

My home was far so I couldn’t stay late every day. Slowly things became bad and one day they just fired me.

That phase already broke my confidence a lot.

Then by pure luck my placement cell again contacted me for an on-campus edtech company. I still remember that day  I felt maybe god is giving me one more chance. I got 7 LPA (6+1) and joined with full motivation.

But again things didn’t go well.

The buddy assigned to train me was from my same batch. He was supposed to give me KT but instead he saw me as competition. He tried everything  highlighting small mistakes, not helping properly, creating negative image.

At the same time company was already running in heavy loss.

One fine day there was a massive layoff. Even the CTO got laid off. My name was already in the list because he was close to the manager.

That day I felt numb. Like I didn’t even have energy to react anymore.

The only positive thing was that the CTO was impressed with my work and later gave me a LinkedIn recommendation. That’s the only thing that still gives me little confidence.

After that I stayed at home for almost 2 months.

Those 2 months were honestly the darkest period of my life.

I was depressed almost daily. Used to overthink at night till morning. There were days I randomly cried without any reason. I kept thinking  did I choose the wrong field, am I not good enough, will my career ever start properly?

Then one day a friend sent me an HR number.

I gave the interview and luckily cleared it.

They knew my situation very well and offered me only 7 LPA  all fixed. No variable, no negotiation. I still accepted because at that point I just wanted stability.

Now it’s been around 10 months here.

I’m working as a sole developer on a project, handling everything myself. Learning a lot, but because of my past experiences I constantly live in fear. Every meeting notification gives me anxiety thinking what if it’s bad news again.

I really want to switch to a better product-based company and put my career on the right track.

But I’m not getting many calls. Resume hardly gets shortlisted. Slowly I lost consistency in DSA again because mentally I feel exhausted.

My biggest fear is  what if I get laid off again?

How will I justify these short stints everywhere? Won’t recruiters think I’m the problem?

Sometimes I feel I’m already late while everyone else is moving ahead.

One more thing that keeps bothering me a lot…

My cousin brother is from a Tier 1 college and he got 37 LPA directly from campus. I’m genuinely happy for him but at the same time it hurts. It constantly makes me feel how far behind I am in my career being from Tier 3.

I keep thinking  will I ever catch up in life? Will I ever reach that pace again or is the gap already too big?

I really want to fight again but right now I feel confused, scared and tired at the same time.

Please guide me like your younger brother.

Is my situation still recoverable?
How should I plan my switch?
Should I restart DSA again?
How do I explain all this honestly in interviews?

Any advice would genuinely mean a lot 🙏

![image.png](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/da6ef485-a160-438c-9dba-f0ef8b11a99c_1769367121.4020755.png)

post.content: Look, you\u2019re not broken. You\u2019re a hard worker, and when something needs to get done, you get it done. I can see it from the recommendation.  That matters more than you think. Stop spinning in your head for a bit and do something physical\u2014go to the gym, sweat, lift, move. Confidence isn\u2019t a thought, it\u2019s a by-product of action.\n\nAnd yeah, if you get laid off again? That would suck. But it won\u2019t end you. You\u2019ve survived worse days than that. You\u2019re still here, still capable, still moving forward. So quit waiting to feel ready\u2014start moving, and let the strength catch up.

I feel you. Do not "explain all this honestly in interviews". Interviewers do not care about your feelings or past experiences. What you need to focus is skill building, DSA and system design. Do not worry about result. Keep focusing on building your resume.

Please stay strong! There\'re many who are from "tier-3" at "tier-1", be consistent and do h/w daily. That\'s all it takes with a bit of luck, yes!\n\nRemember there\'s a lot more to life than fancy work title!
Exact same thing happened with me, still struggling. sometimes luck matters, which we dont have :(
